'''Wren Honoran''' was an elderly [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==Biography==
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], Wren Honoran was [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Attaining the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], Honoran eventually retired from active field work and chose to teach at the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple's]] [[Jedi academy|academy]] as an [[Jedi instructor|instructor]]. Educating his students with his extensive knowledge of [[Politics/Legends|political policy]], Honoran had a knack for survival skills as well.<ref name="TOTJ" />

In addition to his teaching role at the Temple, Honoran would pilot Master-[[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] teams to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Ragoon VI]] to put their survival skills to the test. Like fellow Master [[Winso Bykart]], Honoran would land his ship several [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] from the drop site and disappear into the wilderness, and challenge the teams to track him. On one occasion, Honoran brought Master [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] to the world and engaged in his usual challenge. Kenobi hoped the test would help Skywalker bond with him, since they were having some difficulty connecting. However, the trip took a dangerous turn as Honoran was rendered unconscious by the mysterious [[Granta Omega]], who used him as bait for Kenobi. While Omega escaped from the Jedi when his plan was foiled, Honoran was rescued by his comrades and returned to Coruscant to recuperate.<ref name="TOTJ" />
